name_en: Standing Statue of Yakushi Nyorai (Single-Block Wood Carving)
description_en: A single-block wood-carved standing statue of Yakushi Nyorai classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1969-12-20, and located in Kamiyanagiwatarido.

name_romaji: Yakushi Nyorai Zazō Kakebotoke (Shōhei Jūninen Jūgatsu Mei)
name_en: Hanging Buddha of Seated Yakushi Nyorai (Inscribed 10th Month, 12th Year of Shōhei)
description_en: A hanging Buddha plaque (kakebotoke) of the seated Yakushi Nyorai inscribed with the 10th month of the 12th year of Shōhei, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1969-12-20, and located in Nagisawa.

name_romaji: Den Jūichimen Kannon Zazō Kakebotoke (Kakebotoke Kagamiita)
name_en: Hanging Buddha of the Reputed Seated Eleven-Headed Kannon (Hanging Buddha and Mirror Plate)
description_en: A hanging Buddha plaque (kakebotoke) with mirror plate of the reputed seated Eleven-Headed Kannon, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1969-12-20, and located in Gobōno.

name_en: Seated Statue of Shō Kannon (Hanging Buddha)
description_en: A hanging Buddha plaque (kakebotoke) of the seated Shō Kannon classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1969-12-20, and located in Gobōno.
An archaeological site classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1972-01-07, and located in Niu.
A Bashō haiku monument known as Suzumizuka at Yōsenji, classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1995-01-31, and located in Fumoto-machi.
An archaeological site classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 2002-06-20, and located in Shimoyanagiwatarido.

name_romaji: Ushū Kaidō Namiki Matsu to Sekibutsu
name_en: Roadside Pine Rows and Stone Buddhas of the Ushū Highway
description_en: Roadside pine rows and stone Buddha statues along the Ushū highway, classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 2022-01-27, and located in Ogibukuro.

Kaminohata ceramic ware classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1995-01-31, and located in Nakamachi (on deposit at the Bashō and Seifū Museum of History).

A stone spearhead classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of archaeological materials. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1977-03-28, and located in Gobōno.

An inuzakura cherry tree, 18 meters tall with a trunk girth of 1.76 meters, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1977-03-28, and located in Nobezawa.

A saikachi (honey locust) tree of Mukaiaō, 17.5 meters tall with a trunk girth of 5.5 meters, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1998-01-27, and located in Kamiyanagiwatarido.

A toneriko (ash) tree of Yakushien, 18.7 meters tall with a trunk girth of 4.1 meters, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1998-01-27, and located in Fumoto-machi.

A kōyamaki (umbrella pine) tree of Ryōgoji, 19.4 meters tall with a trunk girth of 3.8 meters, classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1998-01-27, and located in Nobezawa.
Obanazawa gagaku court music classified as an intangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1974-12-26, and located in Obanazawa.
Obanazawa festival music (matsuri bayashi) classified as an intangible cultural property. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1981-05-13, and located in Obanazawa.

A Genna-era land survey register classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Terauchi.

name_en: Pictorial Map of Nobesawa Silver-Mine Village (Fujiya Documents)
description_en: A pictorial map of the Nobesawa silver-mine village from the Fujiya documents, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Nakamachi.

name_en: Tax Ledger of the Nobesawa Silver Mine (Fujiya Documents)
description_en: A tax ledger of the Nobesawa silver mine from the Fujiya documents, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Nakamachi.

name_en: Regulations of the Sekiya Checkpoint (Shibasaki Family Documents)
description_en: Regulations of the Sekiya checkpoint from the Shibasaki family documents, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Toyama.

A gagaku musical score serving as a hichiriki instruction manual, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1975-06-30, and located in Nakamachi.

A haikai-themed sugoroku board game classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1977-03-28, and located in Nakamachi.

A haikai anthology titled Hitotsubashi classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1977-03-28, and located in Nakamachi.

name_en: Letter of Nobesawa Tōtōmi no Kami
description_en: A letter of Nobesawa Tōtōmi no Kami classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1962-11-27, and located in Nakamachi.

An official notice board (kōsatsu) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1977-03-28, and located in Takahashi.

name_en: Village Ordinance of the Tenpō Reforms
description_en: A village ordinance from the Tenpō Reforms classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1977-03-28, and located in Nobezawa.

name_en: Letter of Nobesawa Noto no Kami
description_en: A letter of Nobesawa Noto no Kami classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1997-03-26, and located in Shinmachi-higashi.

name_en: Letter of Nobesawa Noto no Kami (Folded Format)
description_en: A folded-format letter of Nobesawa Noto no Kami classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1997-03-26, and located in Shinmachi-higashi.

A diary attributed to Sōin classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is municipally designated in Obanazawa City, designated on 1998-01-27, and located in Nakamachi.
A stone stupa slab (itabi) classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Ōe Town, designated on 1978-01-17, and located at 661-1 Nukumi.

name_en: Ōe Town Museum of History and Folklore
description_en: The Ōe Town Museum of History and Folklore, a building classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is town-designated in Ōe Town, designated on 1979-08-30, and located at 373-1 Hongōchō.
A Buddhist altar curtain (mitochō) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Ōe Town, designated on 1985-03-23, and located at 373-1 Hongōchō.

name_romaji: Atezawa Jōshu Sakai Naotsugu no Haka
name_en: Grave of Sakai Naotsugu, Lord of Atezawa Castle
description_en: The grave of Sakai Naotsugu, lord of Atezawa Castle, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Ōe Town, designated on 1989-03-08, and located at 525 Hongō-ki.

name_en: Grave of the Wife of Sakai Naotsugu, Lord of Atezawa Castle
description_en: The grave of the wife of Sakai Naotsugu, lord of Atezawa Castle, classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Ōe Town, designated on 1989-03-08, and located at 525 Hongō-ki.
The temple gate of Kokaiin classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is town-designated in Ōe Town, designated on 1989-03-08, and located at 7 Hongō-ki.

A kaya (nutmeg-yew) tree classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1982-03-31, and located in Kurosawa.
A ginkgo tree at Hisenji classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1982-03-31, and located in Ichinono.
A colony of skunk cabbage (mizubashō) classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located in Odo.
A cedar tree classified as a monument (natural monument). It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located in Kodamagawa (Rokutozawa).
